_metadata.description: Transformers Animated characters — TV series (2007-2009, 3 seasons, 42 episodes). Distinctive stylized art with angular, exaggerated proportions.

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader of a small space bridge repair crew. Not yet a Magnus-level commander. Voiced by David Kaye. Commands the ship Orion (Omega Supreme's alt mode)

characters.name: Bumblebee
characters.notes: Fastest Autobot on the team but immature and brash. Voiced by Bumper Robinson. Scanned Captain Fanzone's car for his Earth mode

characters.name: Prowl
characters.notes: Cyber-ninja and loner. Trained under Yoketron. Voiced by Jeff Bennett. Sacrifices himself in the Season 3 finale to reassemble the AllSpark

characters.name: Bulkhead
characters.notes: Gentle giant and space bridge engineer. Clumsy but brilliant with space bridge technology. Voiced by Bill Fagerbakke

characters.name: Ratchet
characters.notes: Crusty, grumpy, war-weary medic. Great War veteran who served alongside Arcee and Omega Supreme. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Sari Sumdac
characters.notes: Techno-organic girl created from a Cybertronian protoform and Isaac Sumdac's DNA. Believed to be human until Season 2. Wields the AllSpark Key. Upgrades to armored form in Season 3. Voiced by Tara Strong

characters.name: Grimlock
characters.notes: Dinobot leader. Originally a theme park animatronic brought to life by Megatron and the AllSpark. Self-designated leader. Voiced by David Kaye

characters.name: Swoop
characters.notes: Dinobot flyer. Created alongside Grimlock and Snarl from theme park animatronics by Megatron and the AllSpark

characters.name: Snarl
characters.notes: Dinobot. Created alongside Grimlock and Swoop from theme park animatronics by Megatron and the AllSpark

characters.name: Ultra Magnus
characters.notes: Supreme Commander of the Autobots and wielder of the Magnus Hammer. Voiced by Jeff Bennett. Critically injured by Shockwave in Season 3

characters.name: Sentinel Prime
characters.notes: Arrogant, xenophobic Elite Guard member. Former academy classmate of Optimus and Elita-1. Becomes acting Magnus after Ultra Magnus is incapacitated. Voiced by Townsend Coleman

characters.name: Jazz
characters.notes: Elite Guard member and cyber-ninja. Trained under Yoketron alongside Prowl. Voiced by Phil LaMarr

characters.name: Blurr
characters.notes: Elite Guard intelligence agent and fastest Autobot. Voiced by John Moschitta Jr., reprising his G1 role. Crushed into a cube by Shockwave/Longarm Prime

characters.name: Jetfire
characters.notes: Elite Guard twin with fire powers. Combines with Jetstorm to form Safeguard. Voiced by Tom Kenny. One of the first flying Autobots, created through Project Safeguard

characters.name: Jetstorm
characters.notes: Elite Guard twin with wind powers. Combines with Jetfire to form Safeguard. Voiced by Phil LaMarr. Created through Project Safeguard alongside his brother

characters.name: Safeguard
characters.notes: Combined form of Jetfire and Jetstorm. Both twins work in complete synchronization. Has a separate vehicle mode from the component parts

characters.name: Cliffjumper
characters.notes: Cybertron Intelligence agent. Works under Longarm Prime (Shockwave in disguise). Voiced by David Kaye. Brief appearances

characters.name: Arcee
characters.notes: Great War veteran and intelligence courier. Carried Omega Supreme's activation codes in her memory. Damaged by Lockdown, memory compromised. Voiced by Susan Blu, reprising her G1 role

characters.name: Omega Supreme
characters.notes: Massive Autobot weapon of last resort from the Great War. His ship form serves as Optimus Prime's crew vessel. Childlike personality. Voiced by Kevin Michael Richardson and Phil LaMarr

characters.name: Rodimus Prime
characters.notes: Leader of Team Athenia. Voiced by Judd Nelson, reprising his G1 Movie role. Encountered on a mission involving Lockdown and oil slick

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Member of Team Athenia. Young and tough. Voiced by Corey Burton. Not the same characterization as G1 Ironhide

characters.name: Hot Shot
characters.notes: Member of Team Athenia. Voiced by Bill Fagerbakke

characters.name: Brawn
characters.notes: Member of Team Athenia. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Red Alert
characters.notes: Member of Team Athenia. Female medic. Voiced by Tara Strong

characters.name: Wasp
characters.notes: Autobot boot camp cadet framed as a Decepticon spy by Shockwave (as Longarm). Imprisoned and driven insane. Later mutated by Blackarachnia into techno-organic Waspinator. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Wreck-Gar
characters.notes: Created from a garbage truck and AllSpark fragment. Impressionable and repeats what others say. Initially confused about his faction. Voiced by Weird Al Yankovic

characters.name: Alpha Trion
characters.notes: Elder Autobot and member of the Autobot High Council. Voiced by Phil LaMarr

characters.name: Yoketron
characters.notes: Ancient cyber-ninja master who trained Prowl and Jazz. Murdered by Lockdown for his collection of protoforms. Voiced by George Takei

characters.name: Highbrow
characters.notes: Autobot High Council member and Cybertron Intelligence liaison. Voiced by David Kaye

characters.name: Perceptor
characters.notes: Head of the Ministry of Science. Speaks in monotone, purely logical manner. Removed his personality components to increase processing power

characters.name: Wheeljack
characters.notes: Ministry of Science member. Helped develop Project Safeguard (the Jetfire/Jetstorm combination)

characters.name: Warpath
characters.notes: Elite Guard member. Brief appearance. Voiced by David Kaye

characters.name: Megatron
characters.notes: Decepticon leader. Manipulated Isaac Sumdac while disembodied for 50 years. Rebuilt body in Season 1 finale. Voiced by Corey Burton. Captured in Season 3 finale

characters.name: Starscream
characters.notes: Treacherous second-in-command. Rendered immortal by an AllSpark fragment. Created clones of himself, each embodying one aspect of his personality. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Skywarp
characters.notes: Starscream clone embodying cowardice. Purple/black color scheme. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Thundercracker
characters.notes: Starscream clone embodying ego/narcissism. Blue color scheme. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Sunstorm
characters.notes: Starscream clone embodying sycophancy. Gold/yellow color scheme. Extremely flattering and obsequious. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Ramjet
characters.notes: Starscream clone embodying deception/compulsive lying. White/maroon color scheme. Everything he says is a lie. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Slipstream
characters.notes: Female Starscream clone. Her specific personality trait is deliberately left ambiguous — she tells Starscream 'Don't ask.' One of the first female Seekers. Voiced by Tara Strong

characters.name: Blitzwing
characters.notes: Triple changer with three distinct personalities: Icy (cold, calculating — jet), Hothead (angry, aggressive — tank), and Random (insane, unpredictable — either). Rebuilt by Blackarachnia. Voiced by Bumper Robinson

characters.name: Lugnut
characters.notes: Fanatically loyal to Megatron. Possesses the P.O.K.E. (Punch Of Kill Everything), a devastating explosive punch. Voiced by David Kaye

characters.name: Lockdown
characters.notes: Bounty hunter with no faction loyalty. Former student of Yoketron before being expelled. Collects weapons and mods from his targets. Later adapted into the main antagonist of Age of Extinction. Voiced by Lance Henriksen

characters.name: Blackarachnia
characters.notes: Formerly Elita-1, an Autobot cadet and friend of Optimus and Sentinel. Mutated into a techno-organic by alien spiders on Archa Seven. Desperate to purge her organic half. Voiced by Cree Summer

characters.name: Shockwave
characters.notes: Decepticon spy who infiltrated the Autobots as Longarm Prime, head of Cybertron Intelligence, for 50 stellar cycles. Fed intel to Megatron. Crushed Blurr into a cube. Voiced by Corey Burton

characters.name: Soundwave
characters.alt_mode: Scion xB-style SUV with musical styling
characters.notes: Originally a toy designed by Megatron as a gift for Sari. Upgraded by the AllSpark Key to become self-aware. Controls other robots through sound. Believes machines should rule over humans. Voiced by Jeff Bennett

characters.name: Laserbeak
characters.notes: Soundwave's minion. Transforms from an electric guitar into a robotic bird. Created alongside Soundwave via AllSpark energy

characters.name: Ratbat
characters.notes: Soundwave's minion. Transforms from a keytar into a robotic bat. Created alongside Soundwave

characters.name: Swindle
characters.notes: Arms dealer and con artist. Sells weapons to anyone who can pay. Stole components from the Autobots while they were incapacitated. Voiced by Fred Willard

characters.name: Scrapper
characters.notes: Constructicon brought to life by an AllSpark fragment. Simple-minded construction worker. Friends with Bulkhead before joining the Decepticons. Does NOT combine into Devastator in Animated. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Mixmaster
characters.notes: Constructicon brought to life by an AllSpark fragment. Enjoys oil and crude substances. Does NOT combine into Devastator in Animated. Voiced by Jeff Bennett

characters.name: Dirt Boss
characters.notes: Constructicon leader. Created from fusion of a forklift, a Headmaster unit, and an AllSpark fragment. Small but domineering, controls others via cerebro-shells. Does NOT combine into Devastator in Animated

characters.name: Strika
characters.notes: Team Chaar commander and Lugnut's consort. Extremely powerful and loyal to Megatron. Voiced by Tara Strong

characters.name: Oil Slick
characters.notes: Team Chaar member. Chemical warfare specialist. Uses cosmic rust and other biological weapons. Voiced by Phil LaMarr

characters.name: Blackout
characters.notes: Team Chaar member. Generates electromagnetic pulses. Appears in Season 3

characters.name: Spittor
characters.notes: Team Chaar member. Uses tongue-like cables and combustible slime as weapons

characters.name: Cyclonus
characters.notes: Team Chaar member. Brief appearance in Season 3. Minimal dialogue

characters.name: Isaac Sumdac
characters.notes: Sari's father and head of Sumdac Systems robotics corporation. Unknowingly reverse-engineered Megatron's technology for decades. Kidnapped by Megatron in Season 1 finale. Voiced by Tom Kenny

characters.name: Captain Fanzone
characters.notes: Detroit police captain. Technophobe who frequently exclaims 'This is why I hate machines!' Reluctant ally of the Autobots. Voiced by Jeff Bennett

characters.name: Porter C. Powell
characters.notes: Corporate schemer who takes over Sumdac Systems when Isaac disappears. Evicts Sari from Sumdac Tower. Voiced by Bumper Robinson

characters.name: Meltdown
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Prometheus Black, former biotech entrepreneur and rival to Isaac Sumdac. Lab accident gave him corrosive acid powers. Obsessed with creating a human-based transformer. Voiced by Peter Stormare

characters.name: Headmaster
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Henry Masterson, disgruntled Sumdac Systems employee. Uses a control unit to hijack robot bodies by replacing their heads. Speaks in gaming/internet slang. Voiced by Alexander Polinsky

characters.name: Nanosec
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Nino Sexton. Uses a prototype speed-enhancing suit that grants superhuman velocity but causes rapid aging. Romantic interest of Slo-Mo. Voiced by Brian Posehn

characters.name: Angry Archer
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Aaron A. Archer. Medieval-themed highwayman armed with tech-enhanced arrows. Member of the Society of Ultimate Villainy. Named after Transformers designer Aaron Archer. Voiced by Jeff Bennett

characters.name: Professor Princess
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Penelope 'Penny' Sutton, Ph.D. Crusades against 'violent boys' toys' using weaponized cute theme gear (magic wand, exploding kitten dolls, flying unicorn). Member of the Society of Ultimate Villainy. Voiced by Kath Soucie

characters.name: Slo-Mo
characters.notes: Supervillain. Real name Samantha Lomow. Former production line worker who weaponized an AllSpark fragment embedded in quartz to create a time-slowing device. Romantic interest of Nanosec. Voiced by Tara Strong

characters.name: Master Disaster
characters.notes: Supervillain. Australian-accented host of illegal street racing show 'Street Demon'. Uses an AllSpark fragment remote to control vehicles and Cybertronians. Voiced by Bill Fagerbakke

characters.name: Colossus Rhodes
characters.notes: Supervillain. Operates a large mech suit. One of Meltdown's mutant experiments. Voiced by Corey Burton
